gaps.title: No general Bandit work-item creation command
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-020 created a CLI-owned work-item creation command that validates explicit slice, chore, and improvement-chore specs before writes, allocates deterministic IDs, appends lifecycle evidence, and links eligible bootstrap gaps as active chores.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit work-item create <spec-path> for one-off work-item starts outside Feature PRD draft-work decomposition.

gaps.title: Bootstrap gaps need repo-native tracking and validation
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-011 created the gap ledger, validation path, default init ledger, and list command.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit validate and bandit gaps list during cold starts, closeout, and future bootstrap-gap routing.

gaps.title: Live CodeRabbit polling and repair orchestration unavailable
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-015 created a CLI-owned fixture-backed live CodeRabbit evidence path with fail-closed missing PR context, missing credential, unavailable provider, request-changes, unresolved finding, stale source, and secret-redaction behavior. Live PR comment repair orchestration and rerun automation remain future integration scope, but this bootstrap gap is resolved for the current live evidence capture contract.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit coderabbit-review live <work-item-id> --pr <number> --fixture <path> for fixture-backed live CodeRabbit evidence until later provider integrations are explicitly scoped.

gaps.title: Stage 4 iterative review evidence-head semantics are undefined
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-015 exposed a recursive Stage 4 loop: each PM disposition and rerun advanced source heads, so Local Qwen kept treating historical evidence-head divergence as a new blocker after accepting implementation behavior.
gaps.next_action: Use the Stage 4 evidence-head policy and landing-readiness behavior from BANDIT-016 for iterative review closeout; remaining Local Qwen hardening is routed to BANDIT-GAP-LANDING-GATE-COMPLEXITY-HARDENING.

gaps.title: Landing gate Stage 4 logic and git diagnostics need hardening
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-017 extracted Stage 4 stale-evaluation and PM-rationale checks from land-check.ts into src/state/landing-stage4.ts, added per-evaluation changed-path git state caching in src/state/git.ts, preserved fail-closed Stage 4 behavior, and landed with Local Qwen pass evidence.
gaps.next_action: Use the BANDIT-017 Stage 4 helper boundary and cached changed-path diagnostics for future landing-readiness work.

gaps.title: Live escalated adversarial reviewer routing unavailable
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-018 created CLI-owned escalated-review routing, configured reviewer profiles, fixture-backed deterministic live-review execution, fail-closed paid-provider setup refusal, landing-gate integration for configured live routes, and operator-approved Opus 4.7 escalated review evidence for this bootstrap chore.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit escalated-review run <work-item-id> for configured escalated-review routing; use operator-approved headless reviewer evidence only when explicitly recorded as bootstrap replacement evidence.

gaps.title: Stage 4 freshness depends on raw git HEAD instead of review subject hash
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-019 added review-subject hash computation, review evidence parsing/template support, a CLI hash command, and landing-readiness behavior that uses review_subject_hash to keep evidence-only commits from forcing repeated Stage 4 review loops while preserving fail-closed source and policy drift behavior.
gaps.next_action: Use review_subject_hash in Stage 4 review evidence for all future work items; raw source_head remains audit metadata, not the primary freshness identity when a hash is present.

gaps.title: No Landing Agent exists yet
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-014 created the repo-native Landing Agent contract, validation path, and local-record landing command that consumes auto-landing eligibility and records landing action evidence while preserving operator-owned UAT and no-remote-operation boundaries.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit land <work-item-id> --action local-record for supported local-record landings; keep live remote merge, push, and deploy behavior out of scope until explicitly specified.

gaps.title: No CLI-owned UAT artifact exists yet
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-012 created the CLI-owned UAT approval artifact, validation path, uat approve command, and stale-UAT land-check enforcement.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit uat approve and land-check UAT freshness checks for future feature-slice landing gates.

gaps.title: No general artifact creation command outside explicit PRD draft-work
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-021 created a narrow CLI-owned artifact creation command for RED evidence, implementation evidence, landing verdicts, and retrospectives from explicit JSON specs, with safe path checks, no-overwrite behavior, canonical work-item validation, lifecycle event evidence, and rollback if lifecycle append fails.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit artifact create <spec-path> for supported evidence artifact families when explicit structured input exists; keep unsupported artifact families manual or future-scope until their input contracts stabilize.

gaps.title: No heartbeat chore-agent exists yet
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-022 created a CLI-governed heartbeat inspect command and policy contract that can inspect eligible low-risk chores and due improvement evaluations from repo-native state while preserving read-only authority, operator-owned UAT boundaries, dirty-worktree refusal, and fail-closed policy validation.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit heartbeat inspect for read-only recurring chore inspection; keep preparation, landing, product UAT, merge, push, deploy, and policy overrides outside heartbeat authority.

gaps.title: Non-blocking review findings need durable chore routing
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-023 added the Stage 4 and Stage 5 contract for Non-Blocking Review Findings: accepted non_blocking reviewer findings require concrete PM disposition and durable follow-up routing, while blocker findings, stale review-subject hashes, CodeRabbit request-changes, unresolved actionable findings, and missing disposition remain fail-closed.
gaps.next_action: Use non_blocking_findings_routing in review evidence when real but safe-to-defer reviewer findings are accepted; evaluate the outcome after the recorded Stage 4 churn window.

gaps.title: No workflow cockpit exists yet
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-024 created the workflow-cockpit boundary artifact that preserves CLI Authority, keeps repo-native artifacts canonical, limits any State Index to rebuildable derived state, maps cockpit read and action surfaces to existing artifacts or approved CLI command families, and defers Phase 8 web implementation plus product/UI tradeoffs until explicit future scope exists.
gaps.next_action: Use docs/design/workflow-cockpit-boundary.md as the authority boundary before any future Phase 8 cockpit implementation work.

gaps.title: CodeRabbit pre-landing loop lacks pre-PR CLI review
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-027 added a CLI-owned pre-PR CodeRabbit review path for local diffs, fixture-backed provider behavior for deterministic tests, fail-closed refusal evidence for missing CLI/auth, provider timeout, malformed output, stale source, and actionable findings, Stage 4 aggregate review consumption of current pre-PR CodeRabbit pass evidence, and preservation of the existing PR-backed live review path.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit coderabbit-review pre-pr <work-item-id> --base <revision> before PR creation when local diffs are the Stage 4 CodeRabbit review subject; evaluate the improvement outcome over the next three Stage 4 Bandit work items and the first later PR-backed landing.

gaps.title: Artifact create landing verdict omits work item field
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-035 repaired the landing_verdict artifact renderer so generated landing verdict Markdown includes the parser-required work_item field, added focused artifact-create tests for parser-compatible landing verdict output, preserved fail-closed landing gate behavior, and recorded Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, Stage 6 retrospective closeout, and gap-ledger disposition.
gaps.next_action: Use generated landing_verdict artifacts with parser-required work_item metadata for future Landing Agent evidence; keep land-check fail-closed if required landing verdict fields are missing.

gaps.title: Stage 6 retrospective artifacts do not require structured improvement mining
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-036 repaired retrospective artifact creation so generated retrospectives require structured_improvement_mining rows with signal, finding, and durable disposition fields, require all ten Stage 6 mining signals, render the mining checklist in repo-native Markdown, and record Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, Stage 6 retrospective closeout, and gap-ledger disposition.
gaps.next_action: Use generated retrospective artifacts with structured improvement-mining evidence for future Stage 6 closeout.

gaps.title: Workflow trials can change policy without predeclared decision guardrails
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-037 makes Workflow Trial and workflow-policy improvement candidates fail closed without predeclared decision criteria, uncertainty or minimum-detectable-effect context, re-evaluation windows, and proxy-risk notes. It also makes policy-changing evaluation decisions require criteria comparison, re-evaluation window evidence, and proxy-risk disposition, exposes guardrail completeness through improvements candidates --json, and records Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, Stage 6 retrospective closeout, and gap-ledger disposition.
gaps.next_action: Use Workflow Trial guardrail metadata and policy-changing evaluation validation for future improvement work.

gaps.title: No first-class lifecycle contract exists for load-bearing skills
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-038 adds a repo-native Skill Lifecycle Contract template, policy artifact, evaluation packets, installed-skill drift evidence, fail-closed lifecycle validation, bandit skill-lifecycle validate [--json], and bandit validate integration without making installed global skill files canonical Bandit state. It records Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, Stage 6 retrospective closeout, and gap-ledger disposition.
gaps.next_action: Use Skill Lifecycle Contract metadata and installed-skill drift evidence before treating load-bearing skills as required stage policy or benchmark variants. Create the next bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-AGENT-EVALUATION-HARNESS before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: No agent evaluation harness exists for model, skill, reviewer, or component benchmarks
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-039 adds a replay-only Agent Evaluation Harness policy, packet and result templates, visible calibration and versioned locked-holdout packet evidence, repo-derived gold-labeled reviewer packet metadata, scorecard metrics, provider-pricing-backed benchmark spend metadata, recurring paid-reviewer promotion thresholds, Skill Lifecycle Contract benchmark-subject identity, fail-closed validation, bandit agent-evaluation validate [--json], and bandit validate integration without changing live work, reviewer routing, model routing, skill policy, cost policy, paid reviewer policy, scheduler behavior, claim or worktree authority, or cockpit UI scope. It records Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, Stage 6 retrospective closeout, and gap-ledger disposition.
gaps.next_action: Use replay-only Agent Evaluation Harness contracts and holdout-backed evidence before treating model, reviewer, skill, or load-bearing component comparisons as policy inputs. Create the next bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-INPUT-QUARANTINE-GATE before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: No structural input quarantine gate exists for external or third-party text
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-040 adds a repo-native Input Quarantine Gate policy, input quarantine and Trusted Source Gate templates, source-class validation, Data-Only External Input handling checks, quarantine boundary evidence checks, bounded Trusted Source Gate metadata validation, Trusted Local Repo Mode limits, bandit input-quarantine validate [--json], and bandit validate integration without implementing layered risk classification, supply-chain policy, dependency or lockfile policy, live reviewer routing, paid reviewer routing, scheduler authority, claim/worktree authority, installed global skill edits, external services, PR/CI workflow, or cockpit UI scope. It records Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, Stage 6 retrospective closeout, and gap-ledger disposition.
gaps.next_action: Use Input Quarantine Gate contracts before release-authorized paths process external or third-party text. Create the next bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-LAYERED-RISK-CLASSIFICATION before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Auto-landing and review depth rely too heavily on smell triggers
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-041 adds a repo-native Layered Risk Classification Gate policy, layered risk template, focused validation, bandit risk-classification validate [--json], bandit validate integration, auto-land-check consumption, explicit release-authorized classification evidence for BANDIT-041, focused tests, Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, Stage 6 retrospective closeout, and gap-ledger disposition. Review-depth, operator-supervision, and auto-landing decisions now consume hard exclusions, never-auto-landable surfaces, blast-radius signals, static-analysis signals, source trust, input quarantine, supply-chain state, and smell-trigger inputs together instead of smell triggers alone.
gaps.next_action: Use Layered Risk Classification Gate contracts before treating review-depth, operator-supervision, or auto-landing decisions as release-authorized. Create the next bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-SUPPLY-CHAIN-GATE before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: No supply-chain gate exists for dependency, workflow, agent-skill, or external tool surfaces
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-042 adds a repo-native Supply-Chain Gate policy, supply-chain evidence template, focused validation, bandit supply-chain-gate validate [--json], bandit validate integration, auto-land-check consumption, explicit release-authorized supply-chain evidence for BANDIT-042, focused tests, Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, Stage 6 retrospective closeout, and gap-ledger disposition. Supply-chain-sensitive dependency manifests, lockfiles, package-manager scripts, CI/release workflows, agent skills, fetched prompts, external tool-install paths, executable generated content, and unknown supply-chain surfaces now require explicit gate evidence before release-authorized auto-landing.
gaps.next_action: Use Supply-Chain Gate contracts before treating dependency, lockfile, package-manager script, CI/release workflow, agent-skill, fetched-prompt, external tool-install, executable generated content, or unknown supply-chain surfaces as release-authorized. Create the next bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-COORDINATION-EVENT-LOG-AUTHORITY before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Coordination projections can become hidden workflow authority
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-043 created a repo-native Coordination Event Log Authority policy, template, validator, CLI command, init/validate wiring, focused tests, review evidence, landing verdict, local-record landing action, and retrospective closeout. Append-only coordination history is now the canonical coordination history for workflow position and accepted coordination context; current-state views, cockpit status, state indexes, SQLite caches, in-flight registries, and derived reports are rebuildable non-authoritative projections, with CAS claim authority preserved as the only future active writable-claim authority exception.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit coordination-authority validate and bandit validate to enforce projection non-authority; create the next queued bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-OPERATOR-FAIL-CLOSED-BOUNDARY before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Fail-closed routing can escalate derivable operational drift to the operator
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-044 created a repo-native Operator Fail-Closed Boundary policy, required operator-boundary template, focused validator, bandit operator-boundary validate [--json], init/validate wiring, smell-trigger alignment checks, mechanical-repair evidence requirements, repair-overreach refusals, focused tests, Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, and Stage 6 retrospective closeout. Operator-blocking fail-closed behavior is now reserved for product direction, UAT, policy, business, explicit cost or risk, irreversible operational-risk, safety-critical release authorization, and genuinely ambiguous scope gates, while derivable operational drift routes to Codex PM or CLI-owned mechanical repair with approved source artifacts, expected-current-state checks, immutable evidence, and post-repair validation.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit operator-boundary validate and bandit validate to enforce operator-owned input boundaries; create the next queued bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-CAS-FENCED-CLAIM-AUTHORITY before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Parallel writable workstreams lack Git refs CAS claim authority, fencing tokens, idempotency keys, and deadlock detection
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-045 created the first repo-native Claim Authority Primitive contract using refs/bandit/* and git update-ref --stdin compare-and-swap transaction semantics, kept .bandit claim files and cockpit/read-model surfaces as projections, added claim-authority policy and template validation, command wiring, deterministic Claim Safety Invariant simulation, fencing-token and idempotency-key requirements, Work-Surface Wait-For Graph cycle refusal, focused tests, Stage 4 review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, and Stage 6 retrospective closeout. True parallel writable workstreams remain blocked until later Git Mutation Serializer and Worktree Bootstrap Contract gates pass.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit claim validate and bandit claim simulate evidence paths to enforce Git refs CAS claim-authority contracts; create the next queued bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-GIT-MUTATION-SERIALIZER before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Parallel worktrees lack a shared Git mutation serializer
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-046 created a repo-native Git Mutation Serializer contract for release-authorized shared .git plumbing mutations, including .bandit/policy/git-mutations.json, docs/templates/git-mutation-serializer.md, docs/git-mutations/BANDIT-046-git-mutation-serializer.json, bandit git-mutation validate --json, init and validate wiring, focused serializer validation tests, immediate claim-owned worktree lock requirements, Repo PM Coordinator-only unlock requirements, timeout and stale-lock fail-closed behavior, serializer/claim-authority separation, and Claim Safety Invariant serializer-failure cleanup handling. Stage 4 pre-PR CodeRabbit and Local Qwen evidence passed with no findings, explicit layered risk-classification and supply-chain gate evidence passed, Stage 5 landing verdict and local-record landing action are recorded, and Stage 6 retrospective closeout is recorded. True parallel writable workstreams remain blocked until later Worktree Bootstrap Contract and scheduler gates pass.
gaps.next_action: Use bandit git-mutation validate --json and the Git Mutation Serializer evidence contract for release-authorized shared .git plumbing changes; create the next queued bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-BOOTSTRAP-MODEL-FAMILY-SEPARATION before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Bootstrap RED/GREEN work lacks enforced model-family and test-edit separation
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-047 created an enforced Bootstrap Model-Family Separation and Test Ownership Boundary gate. The implementation adds model-family separation policy, template, evidence artifacts, validator logic, validate integration, focused tests, Stage 4 CodeRabbit and Local Qwen pass evidence, explicit layered risk-classification and supply-chain gate evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, and Stage 6 retrospective closeout. The gate requires different-model-family Stage 3 routing after Codex-authored or materially edited RED evidence, Claude as the bootstrap Stage 3 Process Adapter path, zero Stage 3 Writer authority over tests, test helpers, fixtures, RED evidence, and acceptance mappings, full invalidation and revert evidence for contaminated Writer attempts, and Codex PM escalation disposition for Claude-authored code while preserving the Bootstrap Orchestration Boundary.
gaps.next_action: Use the BANDIT-047 model-family separation gate for future Stage 2/Stage 3 work; create the next queued bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-FOCUSED-SESSION-CONTEXT before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Cold starts lack focused session context packets
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-048 created a CLI-derived Focused Session Context Packet that is computed from authoritative repo artifacts on demand and marked derived_non_canonical. The implementation adds `bandit session-context current --json|--markdown`, source-linked packet generation, Markdown rendering from the same data model, cold-start evaluation support, focused tests, honest CodeRabbit provider-timeout replacement evidence, Local Qwen non-blocking disposition with durable follow-up candidates, aggregate review evidence, explicit risk-classification and supply-chain evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, and Stage 6 retrospective closeout. The packet exposes current phase, active work item, active bootstrap gap, current stage, exact next action, required operator input, blockers, source hierarchy, required evidence paths, allowed and forbidden actions, and deep-read pointers without introducing a hand-maintained context packet or hidden workflow authority.
gaps.next_action: Use `bandit session-context current --json` as the first compact recovery surface for future Bandit cold starts, then deep-read source pointers as required by AGENTS.md, CLEAN_CODE.md, stage rubrics, or the active work item.

gaps.title: Focused session context fails between closed work item and next active gap
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-049 repaired the Focused Session Context Packet interstitial state after a work item has landed and closed but before the next bootstrap-gap work item is created. The implementation keeps the packet derived_non_canonical, reports last closed work separately from active work, identifies the next queued bootstrap gap and exact next action from repo artifacts, preserves forbidden-action boundaries, and adds focused tests, CodeRabbit pass evidence, Local Qwen non-blocking disposition, aggregate Stage 4 evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, and Stage 6 retrospective closeout.
gaps.next_action: Use `bandit session-context current --json` as the first compact recovery surface in the valid interstitial state between closed work and the next active bootstrap-gap work item.

gaps.title: Cockpit status fails between closed work item and next active gap
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-050 repaired the derived cockpit status interstitial state after a work item has landed and closed but before the next bootstrap-gap work item is created. The implementation keeps cockpit status derived_non_canonical, reports last closed work separately from active work, identifies the next queued bootstrap gap and exact next action from repo artifacts, preserves CLI authority and forbidden-action boundaries, and adds focused tests, CodeRabbit pass evidence, Local Qwen non-blocking disposition, aggregate Stage 4 evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, and Stage 6 retrospective closeout.
gaps.next_action: Use `bandit cockpit status --json` as a derived recovery surface in the valid interstitial state between closed work and the next active bootstrap-gap work item.

gaps.title: Bandit-created worktrees lack a runnable environment bootstrap contract
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-051 created the Worktree Bootstrap Contract command surface, validation behavior, evidence artifacts, and worker-execution refusal boundary for Bandit-created worktrees. The resolved contract covers allowed copied or linked files, setup commands, validation commands, environment-variable references, secret-handling boundaries, expected runtime dependencies, and bootstrap failure evidence before a claim-owned worktree may be treated as runnable.
gaps.next_action: Use the Worktree Bootstrap Contract validation before treating Bandit-created worktrees as runnable; create the next bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-EVENT-DRIVEN-WAKE-SCHEDULER before scheduler execution or unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Parallel work scheduler depends on LLM polling and lacks a wake guarantee
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-052 created the event-driven wake scheduler policy and validation surface. The resolved contract defines event-driven triggers, deterministic non-LLM sweeper requirements, Work Availability Wake Guarantee, one-claim activation, scheduler priority ordering, no-authority trigger boundaries, and fallback-polling preservation until reliable wake behavior is proven.
gaps.next_action: Use the event-driven wake scheduler policy and validation evidence before removing fallback LLM polling for ordinary work discovery; create the next bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-AGENT-OBSERVABILITY-TRACES before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Agent runtime behavior lacks first-class observability traces
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-053 created a repo-native Agent Observability Trace policy, validation command, projection command, representative trace evidence, focused tests, review evidence, landing verdict, local-record landing action, and retrospective closeout while preserving traces and projections as derived non-canonical observability evidence.
gaps.next_action: Use agent-observability trace policy and projection evidence for trace-backed runtime signals; create the next bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-STAGE-CAPABILITY-SCOPE before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Work-item stages lack enforced skill and tool scoping
gaps.rationale: BANDIT-054 created a repo-native Stage Capability Scope policy, validation command, work-item creation spec requirements, generated brief rendering, focused tests, review evidence, landing verdict, local-record landing action, and retrospective closeout for stage-scoped authority roles, required skills, allowed tools, inputs, outputs, evidence, forbidden actions, skill lifecycle contract references, and optional soft budget metadata.
gaps.next_action: Use Stage Capability Scope declarations in future work-item specs and briefs; create the next bootstrap-gap chore for BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE before unrelated Phase 8 work.

gaps.title: Agent execution lacks abnormal-run token and cost failsafes
gaps.rationale: The PRD research reviews identified token economics and multi-agent scheduler cost as unresolved risks, and the operator confirmed budgets should act as failsafes for abnormal runs rather than overly strict caps. Tight caps can force repeated failed attempts and cost more than letting a normal deep review finish. Paid reviewer, paid model, and paid/high-token recurring routes also need current provider-pricing evidence with pricing source, captured date, effective date, freshness or expiry rule, expected per-run cost, spend class, and approval owner before they can be treated as available routing. One-off paid reviewer or model calls before policy promotion must be classified as benchmark/evaluation spend with per-run approval or active spend-class approval, not recurring routing policy.
gaps.next_action: Complete active chore BANDIT-055.

gaps.title: Cockpit trust signals lack artifact-specific evidence freshness SLOs
gaps.rationale: The operator confirmed that cockpit trust signals should use explicit Evidence SLOs and artifact-specific freshness budgets rather than generic confidence badges. CI/tests, CodeRabbit, Local Qwen, escalated review, UAT, landing verdicts, retrospectives, and derived projections need different freshness rules, source links, owners, and staleness reasons.
gaps.next_action: After BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE is resolved, create a bootstrap-gap chore to define artifact-specific Evidence SLOs, freshness budgets, Evidence Trust Signals, staleness reasons, and cockpit/view-model validation for tests, review, UAT, landing, retrospective, and projection evidence.
